Section Title: Plato > What did Plato do? > What was Plato’s family like?
Content:
**Plato** (born 428/427 bce , [Athens](https://www.britannica.com/place/Athens) , Greece—died 348/347, Athens) was an ancient [Greek](https://www.britannica.com/place/Greece) [philosopher](https://www.britannica.com/topic/philosophy) , student of [Socrates](https://www.britannica.com/biography/Socrates) (c. 470–399 bce ), teacher of [Aristotle](https://www.britannica.com/biography/Aristotle) (384–322 bce ), and founder of the [Academy](https://www.britannica.com/topic/Academy-ancient-academy-Athens-Greece) . He is best known as the author of philosophical works of unparalleled influence and is one of the major figures of [Classical antiquity](https://www.britannica.com/event/Classical-antiquity) .

Section Title: Plato > What did Plato do? > What is Plato known for?
Content:
Plato’s most famous work is the *Republic* , which details a wise society run by a philosopher. He is also famous for his dialogues (early, middle, and late), which showcase his metaphysical theory of forms—something else he is well known for. Plato also founded the [Academy](https://www.britannica.com/topic/Academy-ancient-academy-Athens-Greece) , an academic program that many consider to be the first Western university, where he stressed the importance of [science](https://www.britannica.com/science/science) and [mathematics](https://www.britannica.com/science/mathematics) . Because of this, he became known as the “maker of mathematicians.”

Section Title: Plato > Life
Content:
The activity of the older man provided the starting point of Plato’s philosophizing. Moreover, if Plato’s *[Seventh Letter](https://www.britannica.com/topic/Seventh-Letter)* is to be believed (its authorship is disputed), the treatment of Socrates by both the [oligarchy](https://www.britannica.com/topic/oligarchy) and the [democracy](https://www.merriam-webster.com/dictionary/democracy) made Plato wary of entering public life, as someone of his background would normally have done.
